name: Bethpage Black
designer: A.W. Tillinghast (with Joseph H. Burbeck); restored by Rees Jones
green_fee_notes: Black Course 18-hole rates are $70 weekday / $80 weekend for NY State residents and $140 weekday / $160 weekend for non-residents, plus a $5 per-player reservation fee.
notable: Hosted the U.S. Open in 2002 and 2009, the PGA Championship in 2019, and the 2025 Ryder Cup; the 2028 KPMG Women's PGA and 2033 PGA Championship are upcoming
Famous first-tee warning sign recommends the course only for highly skilled golfers
Walking only: golf carts are not permitted on the Black Course
One of five 18-hole courses at Bethpage State Park; Golf Digest has called it the best municipal course in America

confidence.designer: Attribution is debated: NY State Parks credits A.W. Tillinghast; Wikipedia credits superintendent Joseph H. Burbeck as primary with Tillinghast consulting.
confidence.green_fee_tier: Tier straddles $$ (resident $70-$80) and $$$ (non-resident $140-$160); $$ chosen on resident muni pricing. Fee schedule PDF dated 2/27/2025, still posted as current in July 2026.
confidence.tees: Per-hole yardages from BlueGolf; totals for all three tees match USGA NCRDB exactly. Women's ratings exist for White (81.8/155) and Yellow (78.3/155).
confidence.handicap_per_hole: From BlueGolf, corroborated by Golfify; not published on an official NY State Parks source.
